query: What are the major phases, decision points and associated fees, if any, in the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process?
final_answer:
I have comprehensive information from NYISO's official tariff (OATT Attachment HH), technical bulletins, and process overview documents. Let me compile a complete answer.

# NYISO Generation Interconnection Cluster Study Process — Phases, Decision Points & Fees

NYISO replaced its old serial study process (feasibility study → System Reliability Impact Study → Class Year Study, under OATT Attachments S, X, and Z) with a new two-phase **Cluster Study Process** under **OATT Attachment HH ("Standard Interconnection Procedures")**, effective May 21, 2024, to comply with FERC Order No. 2023. The first cycle — the **Transition Cluster Study** — opened its Application Window August 1, 2024. Below is the full sequence of phases, the decision points where developers must commit or exit, and all associated fees/deposits/penalties.

*(Sources: NYISO OATT Att. HH §40.2, §40.9, §40.10 [nyisoviewer.etariff.biz]; NYISO Cluster Study Process Overview figure, Dec. 14, 2023 [nyiso.com]; NYISO Order No. 2023 Compliance Plan, IITF Nov. 2, 2023 & OC Dec. 14, 2023 [nyiso.com]; Transition Cluster Study Application Window Notice, July 1, 2024 [nyiso.com]; SIP FAQ [nyiso.com]; Transition Cluster Study 2024 Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report, Oct. 31, 2025 [nyiso.com]; TEI User's Guide UG-21 v3.0 [nyiso.com].)*

---

## Pre-Application Stage (optional, no formal fee in cluster)
Before a cluster opens, a prospective developer can review NYISO's publicly posted **Heatmap** (estimated available injection capacity) and request a **Pre-Application Report** (§40.4.1–40.4.2). This is informational only and carries no commitment.

---

## Phase A — Application Window
**Duration:** 45 calendar days (75 days for the Transition Cluster). *(Att. HH §40.5; Tech Bulletin.)*

The developer submits an Interconnection Request (or CRIS-Only Request) with all required materials. **Fees due at application:**

| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Application Fee** (non-refundable, cash only) | **$10,000** ($5,000 for CRIS-Only) |
| **Study Deposit** — <80 MW | **$100,000** |
| Study Deposit — ≥80 MW and <200 MW | **$150,000** |
| Study Deposit — ≥200 MW | **$250,000** |
| Study Deposit — CRIS-Only | **$50,000** |

*(Sources: Transition Cluster Application Window Notice, July 1, 2024; Compliance Plan IITF Nov. 2, 2023.)*

- The Study Deposit can be posted as cash, letter of credit, or surety bond (TEI User's Guide).
- NYISO invoices **actual study costs monthly** and only draws on the Study Deposit if a developer fails to pay an invoice. The deposit stays in place for the entire process and is **refunded in full** if, at the end of Phase 2, the developer accepts its cost allocation and posts Security.
- Application also requires: site control demonstration (per Technical Bulletin 260), conceptual one-line diagram, project layout, workable models (short-circuit/steady-state/stability), and NYSRC Reliability Rule B.5 attestation for IBRs >20 MW.

**Validation/Deficiency cure:** NYISO validates within 5 business days (10 for Transition); developers get 10 business days to cure deficiencies (15 for Transition). *(Process Overview figure.)*

---

## Phase B — Customer Engagement Window
**Duration:** 70 calendar days (90 days for the Transition Cluster). *(§40.7; Tech Bulletin.)*

Key activities:
1. NYISO publishes the **Cluster Study Project list** (within 10 business days of window opening).
2. The Connecting Transmission Owner performs a **Physical Infeasibility Screening** — an early check for known issues preventing interconnection at that location.
3. NYISO holds a group **Scoping Meeting** for the cluster.
4. Limited Point-of-Interconnection change allowed within 5 business days of the list posting.

*(Source: Att. HH §40.2.2, §40.7; SIP FAQ.)*

---

## ★ DECISION POINT 1 — Phase 1 Entry Decision Period
**Duration:** 5 business days (§40.7.5).

The developer elects to proceed to Phase 1 (and post **Readiness Deposit 1**) or withdraw.

| Commitment to enter Phase 1 | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Readiness Deposit 1 (RD1)** — a commercial-readiness deposit | **$4,000/MW** |

(RD1 does not apply to CRIS-only projects; NYISO does **not** require a Regulatory Milestone demonstration.)

**Withdrawal Penalty 1 (WP1)** if the project leaves during the Customer Engagement Window or at this decision point:
- **25% of the Study Deposit**, except:
  - **$5,000** if the interconnection is physically infeasible, or
  - **$0** if the project withdraws within 5 business days of the cluster member list posting.

*(Sources: Process Overview figure; Compliance Plan; Att. HH §40.7.6.)*

---

## Phase C — Cluster Study Phase 1
**Duration:** 180 calendar days (190 days in the Transition Cluster; concludes with Operating Committee approval of the **Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report**). *(§40.9.2, §40.10.)*

Conducted **individually** for each project (unless CRIS-only). It identifies the project's direct-connection facilities — the Connecting Transmission Owner's **Attachment Facilities, Distribution Upgrades, and Local System Upgrade Facilities (SUFs) and CTOAFs** — with design, preliminary engineering, and cost/time estimates. *(Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report, Oct. 31, 2025.)*

---

## ★ DECISION POINT 2 — Phase 2 Entry Decision Period
**Duration:** 10 business days (§40.10.8).

The developer elects to proceed to Phase 2 (posting **Readiness Deposit 2**) or withdraw.

| Commitment to enter Phase 2 | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Readiness Deposit 2 (RD2)** | The **greater of** (i) RD1, or (ii) **20% of the Phase 1 cost estimate** for Local SUF and CTOAF |

(Deposits are **cumulative, not additive** — RD2 builds on RD1.)

**Withdrawal Penalty 2 (WP2)** at or before conclusion of Phase 1:
- **50% of the Study Deposit + 10% of RD1**, except **25% of Study Deposit only** if physically infeasible.

*(Sources: Process Overview figure; Compliance Plan IITF Nov. 2, 2023.)*

---

## Phase D — Cluster Study Phase 2
**Duration:** 270 calendar days (concludes with Operating Committee approval of the **Cluster Study Report**). *(§40.9.2, §40.11–40.14.)*

Conducted **collectively** for all cluster projects. NYISO runs the **Cluster Baseline Assessment (CBA)** and **Cluster Project Assessment (CPA)**:
- Identifies **System Upgrade Facilities (SUFs)** and **Distribution Upgrades** needed to meet the **NYISO Minimum Interconnection Standard** (for ERIS).
- For projects requesting CRIS, runs a **Cluster Study Deliverability Study** to identify **System Deliverability Upgrades (SDUs)** under the **NYISO Deliverability Interconnection Standard** (§40.13).
- Allocates upgrade costs among contributing projects.
- If a new SDU is triggered, an **Additional SDU Study** (§40.14) may follow.

*(Source: Att. HH §40.9.1, §40.11–40.14; Phase 1 Summary Report background.)*

---

## ★ DECISION POINT 3 — Final Decision Period
**Format:** Iterative decision rounds (plus an Additional SDU Study Decision Period if applicable). *(§40.15.)*

Each developer decides, through iterative rounds, whether to **accept its Project Cost Allocation** and **pay cash or post Security** for its allocated Attachment Facilities, Distribution Upgrades, SUFs, and SDUs.

| Final commitment | Amount |
|---|---|
| **Security** | **100% of the cost estimate** of the SUFs, SDUs, and CTOAFs identified in Phase 1 and Phase 2 for the project |

- Accepting and posting Security → the **Study Deposit is refunded in full**, and the project proceeds to negotiate a **Standard Interconnection Agreement** and any construction agreements (§40.21).
- Not accepting / not posting Security → project is **withdrawn from the queue** and subject to Withdrawal Penalty 3.

**Withdrawal Penalty 3 (WP3)** at or before conclusion of Phase 2:
- **100% of the Study Deposit + 20% of RD2**, except:
  - **50% of Study Deposit only** if physically infeasible, or
  - **100% of Study Deposit only** if total cost estimate rose **>50%** between Phase 1 and Phase 2 (excluding SDU costs).

**Security Forfeiture (post-acceptance):** If a project accepts its allocation/posts Security and later withdraws, it may forfeit **up to 100% of its Security** if other projects are relying on the upgrades identified for its project. *(§40.15.5, §40.15.)*

*(Sources: Process Overview figure; Compliance Plan; Att. HH §40.9, §40.15.)*

---

## Post-Study — Agreement Negotiation & Construction
Projects that accept cost allocation move to negotiation of the **Standard Interconnection Agreement**, **Standard (Multiparty) Upgrade Construction Agreement**, and an optional Engineering & Procurement Agreement, followed by construction of the upgrades (§40.19–40.21).

---

## How withdrawal-penalty funds are used
Collected withdrawal penalties first **offset the study costs of the remaining cluster projects** that accepted their allocation and posted Security. Any excess beyond actual study costs is used to **offset Rate Schedule 1 costs**. *(Compliance Plan IITF Nov. 2, 2023.)*

---

## Summary Table — Deposits & Penalties by Stage

| Stage / Decision | Deposit Required | Withdrawal Penalty if Exit |
|---|---|---|
| Application | $10,000 App Fee + Study Deposit ($100k/$150k/$250k by size; $50k CRIS-only) | — |
| Phase 1 Entry (DP1) | RD1 = $4,000/MW | WP1 = 25% of Study Deposit ($5k if infeasible; $0 if <5 BD after list) |
| Phase 2 Entry (DP2) | RD2 = greater of RD1 or 20% of Phase 1 cost estimate | WP2 = 50% of Study Deposit + 10% of RD1 (25% of SD if infeasible) |
| Final Decision (DP3) | Security = 100% of SUF/SDU/CTOAF cost estimate | WP3 = 100% of Study Deposit + 20% of RD2 (exceptions for infeasibility / >50% cost increase) |
| Post-acceptance | (Security held) | Up to 100% of Security forfeited if others

steps.content: I'll research the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process, which NYISO has been reforming to align with FERC Order 2023.
steps.reasoning: I need to look into NYISO's cluster study process to understand the major phases, key decision points, and what fees are involved.
